claims.claim: - doi: 10.1093/bja/aei210 - file_origin: pdf - review_state: extracted --- ## Summary This review provides a detailed pharmacological and clinical analysis of serotonin toxicity (serotonin syndrome), with particular focus on interactions between monoamine oxidase inhibitors (MAOIs) and opioid analgesics.

cited_sources.title: Monoamine oxidase inhibitors, opioid analgesics and serotonin toxicity

claims.claim: It establishes that **serotonin toxicity is a dose-dependent toxidrome resulting from elevated intra-synaptic serotonin**, and that **combinations of serotonergic agents acting via different mechanisms—especially MAOIs combined with serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SRIs)—are the primary cause of severe and fatal cases** [oai_citation:0‡Gillman-2005-Monoamine oxidase inhibitors opioid analgesics and serotonin toxicity.pdf](sediment://file_000000005554720aa8cc96ed7a5ac052) The paper identifies specific opioid analgesics with serotonergic properties and distinguishes them from those without such effects, providing a strong mechanistic basis for predicting drug–drug interactions.
claims.mechanism: It establishes that **serotonin toxicity is a dose-dependent toxidrome resulting from elevated intra-synaptic serotonin**, and that **combinations of serotonergic agents acting via different mechanisms—especially MAOIs combined with serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SRIs)—are the primary cause of severe and fatal cases** [oai_citation:0‡Gillman-2005-Monoamine oxidase inhibitors opioid analgesics and serotonin toxicity.pdf](sediment://file_000000005554720aa8cc96ed7a5ac052) The paper identifies specific opioid analgesics with serotonergic properties and distinguishes them from those without such effects, providing a strong mechanistic basis for predicting drug–drug interactions.

claims.claim: --- ## Key Claims ### Claim 1 - claim: Severe serotonin toxicity occurs primarily when monoamine oxidase inhibitors are combined with serotonergic agents acting via different mechanisms - type: mechanism - entities: [MAOI, serotonergic drugs] - directionality: increases serotonin levels synergistically - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 2 - claim: Certain opioid analgesics (meperidine, tramadol, methadone, dextromethorphan) possess serotonin reuptake inhibitor activity and can precipitate serotonin toxicity when combined with MAOIs - type: interaction - entities: [MAOI, meperidine, tramadol, methadone, dextromethorphan] - directionality: increases serotonin toxicity risk - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 3 - claim: Opioids without serotonergic activity (morphine, codeine, oxycodone, buprenorphine) do not precipitate serotonin toxicity when combined with MAOIs - type: interaction - entities: [MAOI, morphine, codeine, oxycodone, buprenorphine] - directionality: no interaction - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 4 - claim: Serotonin toxicity is a dose-dependent spectrum rather than an idiosyncratic reaction - type: mechanism - entities: [serotonin toxicity] - directionality: dose-response relationship - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 5 - claim: Weak serotonin reuptake inhibitors (e.g.

confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 6 - claim: Linezolid and moclobemide function as MAOIs and can precipitate serotonin toxicity when combined with serotonergic drugs - type: interaction - entities: [linezolid, moclobemide, serotonergic drugs] - directionality: increases serotonin toxicity risk - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 7 - claim: Serotonin toxicity is characterized by neuromuscular hyperactivity, autonomic instability, and altered mental status - type: mechanism - entities: [serotonin toxicity] - directionality: clinical syndrome - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ## Mechanisms Described - Monoamine oxidase inhibition → decreased serotonin breakdown - Serotonin reuptake inhibition → increased synaptic serotonin - Serotonin release (in some agents) → further elevation of serotonin - Synergistic pharmacodynamic interaction between MAOIs and SRIs - Dose-dependent accumulation of serotonin leading to toxicity --- ## Clinical / Practical Guidance - Avoid combining MAOIs with serotonergic opioids (especially meperidine, tramadol, methadone, dextromethorphan) - Avoid combining MAOIs with SSRIs, SNRIs, or other SRIs - Prefer non-serotonergic opioids (morphine, oxycodone, etc.) when analgesia is required - Recognize early signs of serotonin toxicity (clonus, hyperreflexia, agitation) - Treat severe cases with 5-HT2A antagonists and supportive care [oai_citation:1‡Gillman-2005-Monoamine oxidase inhibitors opioid analgesics and serotonin toxicity.pdf](sediment://file_000000005554720aa8cc96ed7a5ac052) --- ## Contraindications / Risks - MAOI + SRI combinations → high risk of severe serotonin toxicity - MAOI + serotonergic opioids → potentially fatal interactions - MAOI + serotonin releasers (e.g.
